ogTitle: North Bodallin vs North Boyanup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Bodallin and North Boyanup.
heading: North Bodallin vs North Boyanup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Bodallin, Western Australia and North Boyanup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to North Bodallin (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Bodallin or North Boyanup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Bodallin has a flood risk score of 56/100, while North Boyanup scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Bodallin scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. North Boyanup scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Bodallin or North Boyanup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
